torch_pattern: x1 = x[..., :split]; x2 = x[..., split:]  # sibling slices of one producer, e.g. u_top/u_bot row split of u_stem in test ([0,0,0,0]->[1,1,half,cols] and [0,0,half,0]->[1,1,rows,cols]); q_nope/q_rope, w1/w3, q/kv_nope/kv_rope channel splits

config_template.notes: N sibling ttnn.slice ops over the same parent tensor partitioning one dim (rows or channels); parent typically deallocated immediately after all branch slices. Branch memory_configs may differ (e.g. q_nope interleaved, q_rope height-sharded).

placement_observations.size_descriptor: row-split halves [1,1,{ROWS}/2,{COLS}] of a sharded layer_norm output

memory_config.shard_spec_template: shards['left'] (height-sharded spec sized to half rows)

placement_observations.source: unit test: u_top = ttnn.slice(u_stem, ..., memory_config=shards['left']); u_bot = ttnn.slice(u_stem, ..., memory_config=shards['left']); ttnn.deallocate(u_stem)
deepseek MLA decode: tt_q_rope = ttnn.slice(tt_q, [0,0,0,qk_nope_head_dim], ..., **q_rope_slice_config) while sibling tt_q_nope slice stays L1 interleaved

placement_observations.size_descriptor: decode fused q/kv split branches [1,1,{BATCH},{SUB_DIM}], BATCH<=32
placement_observations.source: deepseek MLA decode benchmark: tt_q / tt_kv_nope / tt_kv_rope = ttnn.slice(tt_q_kv, ..., memory_config=ttnn.L1_MEMORY_CONFIG) x3 then tt_q_kv.deallocate(True)

placement_observations.size_descriptor: prefill fused q/kv split branches [1,{BATCH},{SEQ},{SUB_DIM}] with large SEQ
placement_observations.source: deepseek MLA prefill: tt_q/tt_kv_nope/tt_kv_rope slices use no memory_config (default DRAM interleaved); sibling rms_norm consumer uses memory_config=ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG
